hard.next_step: Talked with Karen on May 11th - Doiing a deep dive on another eternal I/O provider. Did not indicate which one. Suggested a touch base in 2 months - and has accepted a 20th July Connect - outcome could very well be that we re-engage. Karen Pagorek shared that they are re-assessing prioritiies and that the POC will not mo e forward. Havbe reached out to set a phone call to fully understand ramifications (on hold, in-house build, or competitive loss) - plus try for an onsite while in Charlotte.
hard.ais_why: On the 12/18/2025 call, champion Karen Pagorek pushed Arthur to define autonomous and agentic terms more precisely and pressure-tested ROI realism on the $48M savings claim. The offsetting signal is that the three-prong AI POC was paused on 5/11/26; active autonomous-sourcing dialogue offsets the pause, but the pause is why this stays in the cautious band.

forecast_read.reason: Best Case is not supported: the buyer paused the pilots on 11 May 2026 to deep-dive an undisclosed alternative, said the POC will not move forward while priorities are re-assessed, no CFO or budget path is engaged, and the close date has slipped three times. Eduardo Viera confirmed on 12 Jun 2026 only that Zycus is one of the options being evaluated. This is a healthy relationship with a paused evaluation, so it is a conviction problem, not yet a dead deal: hold at Pipeline until the 20 Jul call restores a working plan.
forecast_read.math: 228 days to close (2027-02-22). Deal created 2025-06-06 (13 months old); in Shortlisted since 2025-12-05 (~7 months, far beyond a normal Shortlisted dwell). Close date slipped 2026-03-27 to 2026-06-26 (26 Feb), to 2026-09-30 (4 May), to 2027-02-22 (11 May): roughly 11 months of cumulative slip. To make Feb 2027 the buyer must re-open the evaluation at the 20 Jul call and reach Vendor Selected by roughly Oct-Nov 2026.

metrics.narrative: The buyer's own funding logic is quantified: Eduardo Viera said on 30 Sep 2025 that the 2024 investment is 'backing this investment with an estimate on return... hard savings or hard numbers' from incremental cost reduction and elimination of manual work. But Zycus's value case is not yet accepted: on 18 Dec 2025 Karen Pagorek called the projected 40% sourcing-cycle-time reduction 'pretty optimistic' and asked for benchmarks against John Deere's actual environment. Arthur offered a 70-80 data-point value-engineering comparison and a sample sheet, promising a care package by about 5 Jan 2026, but nothing in the record confirms the buyer validated it before the 11 May 2026 pause. The rep-logged note that the ANA and Spend POC 'was positive' (April 2026) is rep-reported, not buyer-confirmed.

economic_buyer.narrative: Eduardo Viera (Global Director, Supply Management Indirect Materials and Services; primary Executive Sponsor contact role) is the person who secured the 2024 funding and framed the make-versus-buy direction in a direct 1:1 on 30 Sep 2025, and he re-surfaced on 12 Jun 2026 with a written reply: 'We are evaluating options and Zycus is one of those. Karen will be sharing more details in your next call.' That is identified but passive. CFO Joshua Jepsen carries an Executive Sponsor contact role yet has never appeared on a call in 13 months, so the ultimate budget authority is unengaged and the commercial approval path is unconfirmed.
sources: Email reply from Eduardo Viera, 2026-06-12 (SF task)

decision_criteria.narrative: The criteria that matter were voiced on the 18 Dec 2025 proposal call: a realistic, benchmark-backed cost justification (Karen: 'that seems pretty optimistic... I need to reset based on what our current environment is'), clean integration with the internally built iProcure and the SAP stack, precise definitions separating e-auctions from autonomous sourcing, and supplier experience with autonomous negotiation (Deere's past evaluations 'haven't gotten great feedback in all areas'). No formal RFP or scored criteria document exists; the evaluation is champion-run and, since 11 May 2026, includes an undisclosed alternative provider.

decision_process.narrative: Eduardo said on 30 Sep 2025 that Deere was not running a competitive tender ('I don't think that we are looking at a competitive') and framed a phased make-versus-buy assessment with an on-site phase-3 workshop. The live process since 11 May 2026 is a buyer-set two-month deep dive on an alternative provider ending at the accepted 20 Jul 2026 call, where Karen will share the outcome per Eduardo's 12 Jun email. The rep's own 28 Jan 2026 note names the path to agreement as 'through Karen Pagorek and Eduardo Viera.' Approval mechanics above Eduardo (CFO, procurement of software) have never been mapped.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting has never been discussed beyond the 18 Dec 2025 budgetary proposal, which Arthur explicitly framed as 'not anywhere near a final commercial proposal.' No MSA, order form, SOW, procurement or legal contact has entered the record. This is expected for a Shortlisted deal that is currently paused, but it means there is no paper runway toward the Feb 2027 close yet.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is confirmed in the buyer's own words across two threads. Karen Powers (24 Jun 2025) described the homegrown TCS quoting stack and the 'deere-izing' customization culture as unsustainable for AI-speed transformation ('if you really want to go fast and transform from an AI standpoint, is building everything yourself the right direction? Probably not'). Eduardo (30 Sep 2025) tied the program to eliminating manual work and hard-savings targets, wanting a partner to 'accelerate what we don't have in our portfolio.' Karen Pagorek (18 Dec 2025) added dissatisfaction with a prior spend-analytics vendor's accuracy and turnaround, and poor supplier feedback from earlier autonomous-negotiation trials.

champion.narrative: Karen Pagorek (Global Strategic Sourcing Manager, Evaluator role) has been the working engine of the deal since late 2025: she ran the 18 Dec 2025 proposal deep-dive with detailed prepared questions, scheduled and staffed the 31 Mar 2026 POV call with her team, and said she would 'discuss a little further here with Eduardo' on pilots. But on 11 May 2026 she told Arthur the POC 'will not move forward' while she deep-dives an alternative provider she declined to name, and set a two-month distance. She is engaged and candid, but right now she is an honest evaluator, not an advocate fighting for Zycus, and she holds no budget authority.
sources: SF task: Formal Submit POV call, 2026-03-31

competition.narrative: The live threat is an intake-and-orchestration provider the buyer declined to name on 11 May 2026, whose deep dive directly paused the Zycus pilots; its identity is the single most important unknown on the deal. Beneath it sits the durable incumbent: Deere's funded in-house build (TCS quoting plus OpenAI/Ultrametrics/Worldlink partnerships), which Eduardo frames as make-versus-buy rather than either-or. GEP was being countered on spend analytics per the rep's 24 Feb 2026 plan. Oracle and SAP Ariba sit in the Salesforce competitor field with no call corroboration, though Eduardo warned on 30 Sep 2025 that Deere's internal SAP IT team 'are going to look at us as competition in a major way.'

critical_signals.text: An intake-and-orchestration provider the buyer declined to name is being deep-dived since 11 May 2026, and that review directly paused the Zycus pilots, with the outcome due on the 20 July call.
On the 11 May call Karen Pagorek said the POC will not move forward while John Deere re-assesses priorities, and set a two-month distance before the accepted 20 July touch base.
Eduardo Viera confirmed in writing on 12 June that Zycus is still one of the options being evaluated and that Karen will share details on the next call, so the door is open, not closed.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A well-qualified, AI-hungry buyer paused the Zycus pilots on 11 May 2026 to evaluate an undisclosed alternative, leaving Zycus formally in the field but with no buyer-voiced preference, a passive economic buyer, and a close date that has slipped three times. Everything now hinges on the buyer-accepted 20 July re-engagement call with Karen Pagorek.

win_position.text: The buyer paused the ANA and spend pilots on 11 May 2026 to deep-dive an alternative provider they declined to name, and Karen Pagorek said the POC 'will not move forward' while priorities are re-assessed.

There is no buyer-voiced preference: Eduardo Viera's 12 Jun 2026 reply says only 'we are evaluating options and Zycus is one of those,' and the business case Karen called 'pretty optimistic' on 18 Dec 2025 was never buyer-validated.
Executive access is thin: CFO Joshua Jepsen has never joined a call in 13 months, and Eduardo Viera's last working session was 30 Sep 2025, with only a brief email reply since.

Zycus remains formally in the evaluation with a buyer-accepted 20 Jul re-engagement call, and the working relationship with Karen Pagorek is candid and intact (she personally proposed the two-month check-in on 11 May).

The fit itself is strong when the deal is live: Eduardo confirmed funded, hard-savings-backed AI transformation (30 Sep 2025) and Karen pressure-tested agentic definitions and pilot design in depth (18 Dec 2025).

deal_momentum.text: No genuine buyer engagement in the last 14 days; the only July touch is a 2 Jul outbound call to Kathleen Johnson that did not connect.
Eduardo Viera replied on 12 Jun 2026 confirming Zycus is still in the evaluation, and a buyer-accepted future meeting (20 Jul touch base with Karen) is on the calendar.
The evaluation is paused: no deliverable has moved since the POC details landed on 31 Mar 2026, and the buyer set a two-month cooling window on 11 May rather than a working next step.
The close date was pushed a third time on 11 May 2026 (30 Sep 2026 to 22 Feb 2027), roughly 11 months of cumulative slip from the original 27 Mar 2026.

customer_commitment.text: Karen Pagorek herself suggested the two-month check-in and accepted the 20 Jul call (11 May 2026), and Eduardo Viera put in writing on 12 Jun that Karen will share the evaluation details there.
Beyond that single accepted call, the buyer owes nothing and has scheduled nothing: no data, no workshop, no pilot resumption is committed.

deal_risk.text: Competitive displacement risk is live: the unnamed provider's deep dive is what paused the Zycus pilots, and Deere's default is its own funded build ('deere-izing', Karen Powers, 24 Jun 2025).
Single-threaded exposure: since May 2026 every touch runs through Karen Pagorek; Gia Duke could not be reached (two failed calls 3 Jun) and the CFO has never engaged.

Forecast risk: Best Case with a paused evaluation and no budget path is not defensible; if the 20 Jul call does not re-open the evaluation, the honest outcome set is on-hold, in-house build, or competitive loss (the rep's own framing, 11 May 2026).

customer_preference.evidence: Karen Pagorek paused the pilots on 11 May 2026 to deep-dive an alternative provider, and Eduardo Viera's 12 Jun 2026 email says only 'we are evaluating options and Zycus is one of those' - Zycus is in the field, not preferred.

business_case.evidence: Buyer funding is quantified in hard-savings terms (Eduardo Viera, 30 Sep 2025), but Karen Pagorek challenged Zycus's ROI claims as 'pretty optimistic' on 18 Dec 2025 and the promised 70-80 data-point value-engineering benchmark was never confirmed delivered or accepted before the 11 May 2026 pause.

deal_movement.summary: Since the 11 May 2026 pause the deal has moved only at the edges: the close date was pushed to 22 Feb 2027 (11 May), Eduardo Viera confirmed by email on 12 Jun that Zycus remains in the evaluation, and the buyer-accepted 20 Jul re-engagement call is booked. Rep attempts to widen the thread (Gia Duke 3 Jun, Kathleen Johnson 2 Jul) did not connect.

items.change: Close date pushed from 2026-09-30 to 2027-02-22 (third slip, cumulative ~11 months)
Karen Pagorek: POC will not move forward; priorities being re-assessed; two-month touch base proposed and 20 Jul call accepted
Eduardo Viera replied in writing: 'We are evaluating options and Zycus is one of those. Karen will be sharing more details in your next call.'
Outbound calls to Gia Duke (3 Jun, twice) and Kathleen Johnson (2 Jul) did not connect - multi-threading attempts unsuccessful

day_summary.overall: The last day with real deal activity was 12 June, when Eduardo Viera replied to the deal owner's status note and confirmed in writing that John Deere is evaluating options with Zycus among them, and that Karen Pagorek will share the details on the next call. The deal owner acknowledged and thanked him for the transparency. Since then the deal has been quiet on the buyer side ahead of the accepted 20 July touch base; a 2 July call attempt to Kathleen Johnson did not connect.

items.name: Deal owner acknowledgement
items.summary: Arthur thanked Eduardo for the open engagement and kept the July re-engagement framing

competitive_position.summary: The single strongest current threat is an intake-and-orchestration provider the buyer declined to name on 11 May 2026, whose deep dive directly paused the Zycus pilots and whose verdict lands at the 20 Jul call. Behind it sits the durable incumbent, John Deere's funded in-house build (TCS quoting plus OpenAI/Ultrametrics/Worldlink partnerships), which Eduardo frames as make-versus-buy. GEP was being actively countered on spend analytics in Feb 2026. Oracle and SAP Ariba are field-logged only, with no call corroboration in 13 months, though Deere's internal SAP IT team should be expected to resist Zycus on integration turf.

competitors.name: Unnamed intake & orchestration (I/O) provider
competitors.quote: Doing a deep dive on another eternal [external] I/O provider. Did not indicate which one.
competitors.how_we_win: Get the name and the review's trigger on the 20 Jul call, then counter an intake overlay with full-suite depth (sourcing, CLM, supplier risk that overlays lack) and the already-delivered ANA/spend POC results plus the outstanding benchmark validation.

competitors.name: Do nothing / in-house build (TCS homegrown + OpenAI, Ultrametrics, Worldlink partnerships)
competitors.quote: Historically, Deere has definitely been one where we like to customize our tools to match our processes... we call it deere-izing.
competitors.source: Avoma: Zycus Overview / Demo, 2025-06-24 (Karen Powers); reinforced by Eduardo Viera 2025-09-30
competitors.how_we_win: Keep Eduardo's own make-versus-buy framing (30 Sep 2025): modular pick-and-choose acceleration of what they have not built, never a rip-and-replace of the internal stack.

competitors.name: GEP
competitors.quote: Pilot Spend Analysis to counter GEP
competitors.how_we_win: Land spend-analytics proof with actionable insights, not dashboards - the buyer's own stated preference on 18 Dec 2025 - and exploit their dissatisfaction with a prior spend vendor's accuracy and turnaround.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: They are going to look at us as competition in a major way as you probably would expect.
competitors.source: Competitors__c; internal-SAP-team context from Avoma: Arthur/Eduardo 1:1, 2025-09-30
competitors.how_we_win: No active Ariba evaluation evidenced in 13 months; pre-empt the internal SAP IT team's resistance by bringing them into the architecture conversation, as Eduardo himself proposed.

competitors.name: Oracle Procurement Cloud
competitors.source: Competitors__c (field-logged; no corroborating call evidence in any read source)

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Zycus is aligned on modular make-versus-buy acceleration and integration architecture, partially aligned on ROI credibility (challenged, not yet re-proven), and exposed on supplier experience with autonomous negotiation, where Deere carries scar tissue from prior evaluations.

items.criterion: Modular, pick-and-choose acceleration of the in-house build (make-versus-buy)
items.quote: I was excited to see that the possibility could be through modular architecture... what pieces of that we could pick and complement what we are working on.
That seems pretty optimistic... or I need to reset based on what our current environment is.

items.criterion: Integration with the internally built iProcure and SAP stack
items.quote: This time, also, I would like to bring the SAP technical team into that conversation.
We have done some evaluations with that, and we haven't gotten great feedback in all areas.

best_practice_check.summary: The deal's win strategy has three levers before the 20 Jul call: neutralize the unnamed rival, convert ROI skepticism into validated proof, and rebuild a power line above the single working thread.
flags: Competitive counter: identify the unnamed intake-and-orchestration provider before 20 Jul and prepare a full-suite-versus-overlay differentiation brief, so the call re-opens the evaluation instead of just receiving a verdict.

Credibility: deliver the value-engineering benchmark pack (70-80 data points) and re-offer the Tenneco Federal-Mogul reference call - the ROI-realism objection from 18 Dec 2025 was never closed and is the buyer's stated decision lens.

Power and multi-threading: every touch since May 2026 runs through Karen Pagorek; use Eduardo Viera's 12 Jun reply as the opening for a direct follow-up after the 20 Jul call, and keep working the Gia Duke / Kathleen Johnson threads that did not connect in June-July.

champion_strength.summary: Karen Pagorek drove the deal for six months (proposal deep-dive 18 Dec 2025, POV call she scheduled 31 Mar 2026) but paused the pilots on 11 May 2026 to review an alternative she declined to name and set a two-month distance. She remains candid and accepted the 20 Jul call, but she is currently an evaluator running a bake-off, not an advocate.

expansion_context.note: New-business pursuit; no sibling Closed-Won evidence in the read record.

scope_change.from: Full S2P budgetary proposal spanning iSource, autonomous sourcing (ANA), spend analytics, Merlin Intake + eProcurement, iSaaS (18 Dec 2025)
scope_change.to: Three free proof-of-value pilots (sandbox iSource RFX, ANA on an MRO bundle, 3-month spend cube) delivered 31 Mar 2026, then paused 11 May 2026
scope_change.detail: Karen kept AP/invoicing out of her scope on 18 Dec 2025; the working scope narrowed to sourcing, autonomous negotiation, and spend analytics before the pause. The full-suite frame can return only if the 20 Jul call re-opens the evaluation.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning is the deal's strongest asset: the buyer pressure-tested agentic definitions and pilot design in depth (Karen Pagorek, 18 Dec 2025) and Deere's whole program is a funded AI transformation. The 11 May pause is a prioritization event, not an AI-fit rejection.

ai_fit_signal.summary: John Deere is a genuinely AI-forward buyer: funded AI transformation with hard-savings targets, OpenAI/Ultrametrics/Worldlink partnerships, an internal agentic build, and a buyer who interrogates autonomous-sourcing mechanics rather than asking what AI is. The risk is that their AI hunger feeds the in-house build.

CFO Joshua Jepsen never engaged in 13 months; Eduardo Viera passive since 30 Sep 2025 apart from a 12 Jun 2026 email; single-threaded through Karen Pagorek since May 2026

ROI claims challenged as 'pretty optimistic' (18 Dec 2025); benchmark validation and the Tenneco Federal-Mogul reference call are unconfirmed as delivered, and Deere carries poor supplier feedback from prior autonomous-negotiation evaluations

The offsetting signals are Eduardo's written confirmation that Zycus is still in the evaluation (12 Jun) and the buyer-accepted 20 Jul call.

⚠️ No buyer-voiced preference: Eduardo Viera wrote on 12 Jun 2026 only that 'we are evaluating options and Zycus is one of those.' ► Re-anchor on the delivered ANA/spend POC and land the promised benchmark pack before 20 Jul.

⚠️ Executive access is thin: CFO Joshua Jepsen has never joined a call in 13 months and Eduardo Viera's last working session was 30 Sep 2025. ► Ask Eduardo for a direct thirty minutes after the 20 Jul call.

⚠️ The business case is unproven: Karen called the 40% cycle-time claim 'pretty optimistic' on 18 Dec 2025 and the promised value-engineering pack is unconfirmed as delivered. ► Deliver it with the Tenneco Federal-Mogul reference offer before the call.

✅ Zycus remains formally in the evaluation with a buyer-accepted 20 Jul re-engagement call, and the working relationship with Karen Pagorek is candid and intact (she proposed the check-in herself on 11 May).

✅ The underlying fit is strong: a funded, hard-savings-backed AI transformation (Eduardo Viera, 30 Sep 2025) and a buyer who pressure-tests agentic mechanics in depth (Karen Pagorek, 18 Dec 2025). Note: the recorded Best Case forecast over-positions the deal; the evidence supports Pipeline until the 20 Jul verdict. ► Correct the forecast category to Pipeline in Salesforce.
